chunks.text: Edward Lee receives IEEE Technical Committee on Real-Time Systems Outstanding Technical Achievement and Leadership Award - EECS at Berkeley Prof. Edward Lee has been selected to receive the IEEE Technical Committee on Real-Time Systems Outstanding Technical Achievement and Leadership Award . This award is given to recognize individuals for their outstanding technical achievement and leadership. Prof. Lee’s current research interests center on design, modeling, and analysis of embedded, real-time computational systems. He is the Director of the nine-university TerraSwarm Research Center , a director of the Berkeley Center for Hybrid and Embedded Software Systems (CHESS), and the Director of the Berkeley Ptolemy Project . Categories Faculty Honors Research View Open Faculty Positions
